growth_records.strain: not applicable (enrichment culture from Wadden Sea intertidal sand flat sediment, extracted cells)
growth_records.medium: Filter-sterile synthetic seawater medium with cyclic (tidal) O2 pulses, continuous chemostat feed

up.method: reported (stated as an upper-bound average cellular O2 uptake rate for the continuous culture, derived by the authors from a diffusion-flux calculation assuming complete O2 depletion at the cell/aggregate surface; see their Fig. 4e)

growth_records.cite: Chen et al. Impacts of chemical gradients on microbial community structure. The ISME Journal (2017). DOI:10.1038/ismej.2016.175. [PMC5363838]
growth_records.snippet: ...medium was continuously supplied at a dilution rate of 0.26 per day... The chemostat was run without external interruption for over 3 months, approximately 37 microbial generations at the dilution rate applied (0.26 p
growth_records.notes: growth_rate_per_h = dilution rate D, using standard chemostat steady-state theory (mu = D for any population that persists without washout, over ~37 generations of stable operation): reported D = 0.26 per day, converted to per hour: 0.26/24 = 0.010833 h-1. doubling_time_h = ln2/mu = 0.6931/0.010833 = 63.98 h. This is a COMMUNITY-level rate applying jointly to the 5 coexisting dominant clades (not a single axenic species measurement); organism field lists all identified dominant taxa. Oxygen uptake rate (20 mmol/g protein/h) is reported directly in the paper (not gDW-normalized; units are per gram of cell protein) and represents an upper-bound estimate for the mixed community, not species-resolved.
